description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural, unfinished appearance and visible grain patterns. The handle is thick and ergonomically shaped, tapering slightly towards the end for a secure grip. The axe head is made of dark, weathered metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade has a single, slightly convex edge and a pronounced beard, giving it a traditional utilitarian look. The head is mounted onto the handle using a classic wedged fitting, with no visible modern fasteners or embellishments. The overall appearance suggests a tool designed for chopping wood or similar tasks, commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A traditional hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, dark metal blade, designed for chopping tasks. The tool has a weathered, utilitarian look with a wedged head and ergonomic grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the alignment of the handle with the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the end of the handle and the attachment point of the head.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a convex edge, pronounced beard, and flat poll; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting with the blade head mounted onto the handle.
